title: URL Shortener
description: Design a scalable URL shortening service that creates short, unique links and redirects users to original URLs

coreRequirements.label: URL Shortening
coreRequirements.description: Service must accept long URLs and generate short, compact aliases
examplePhrases: Users can submit a long URL and receive a shortened version
System generates unique short links for any URL

coreRequirements.label: URL Redirection
coreRequirements.description: When accessing a short URL, users should be redirected to the original long URL
examplePhrases: Accessing the short URL redirects to the original destination
System performs HTTP 301/302 redirect to long URL

coreRequirements.label: Uniqueness Guarantee
coreRequirements.description: Each generated short URL must be unique to prevent collisions
examplePhrases: Every short URL is guaranteed to be unique
Use hashing or sequential IDs to prevent collisions

optionalRequirements.label: Custom Aliases
optionalRequirements.description: Users can optionally specify custom short URLs instead of system-generated ones
examplePhrases: Users can request custom aliases like example.com/my-link
Support vanity URLs chosen by users

optionalRequirements.label: Link Expiration
optionalRequirements.description: Short URLs can have optional expiration dates after which they become invalid
examplePhrases: Links can expire after a specified time period
Support TTL for temporary URLs

optionalRequirements.label: Analytics & Metrics
optionalRequirements.description: Track and provide metrics on link usage such as click counts and geographic data
examplePhrases: Track number of clicks per short URL
Collect analytics on geographic location and referrers

optionalRequirements.label: User Authentication
optionalRequirements.description: Support user accounts for managing personal short URLs
examplePhrases: Users can create accounts to manage their URLs
API key authentication for programmatic access

optionalRequirements.label: URL Management & Deletion
optionalRequirements.description: Users should be able to view, edit, and delete their created short URLs
examplePhrases: Users can delete URLs they no longer need
Dashboard to view and manage all created links

qualitativeAspects.label: Performance
qualitativeAspects.description: How fast should the system respond? Consider redirect latency and overall responsiveness.
examplePhrases: Redirects should be very fast to provide good user experience
Low latency is critical for user satisfaction

qualitativeAspects.label: Scalability
qualitativeAspects.description: How much traffic should the system handle? Think about growth and load patterns.
examplePhrases: System should handle high traffic volumes
Must scale to accommodate growth

qualitativeAspects.label: Availability
qualitativeAspects.description: How reliable and available should the system be?
examplePhrases: System should have high availability with minimal downtime
Reliability is important for user trust

qualitativeAspects.label: Consistency & Reliability
qualitativeAspects.description: How should the system handle data consistency and reliability?
examplePhrases: URL mappings must be reliable and not lost
System should ensure data consistency

requiredEndpoints.purpose: Create a new short URL from a long URL
requiredEndpoints.exampleNotes: POST with { url: string, customAlias?: string, expiresAt?: timestamp }. Returns { shortId: string, shortUrl: string }. Errors: 400 (invalid URL), 409 (alias taken).

requiredEndpoints.purpose: Redirect to the original long URL
requiredEndpoints.exampleNotes: GET /{shortId} returns HTTP 301/302 redirect to original URL. Returns 404 if not found or expired.

requiredEndpoints.purpose: Retrieve analytics and metrics for a short URL
requiredEndpoints.exampleNotes: Returns { clicks: number, lastAccessed: timestamp, referrers: [] }. Requires auth if user-specific.

optionalEndpoints.exampleNotes: DELETE requires authentication. Returns 204 on success, 403 if unauthorized.

optionalEndpoints.purpose: List all URLs for authenticated user
optionalEndpoints.exampleNotes: Returns paginated list of user's URLs. Requires authentication.
PUT to update expiration or destination. Requires auth.

feedbackTemplates.missing: You need a Service component to handle URL creation and redirection logic.
feedbackTemplates.present: Good! Service component is present to handle core business logic.

feedbackTemplates.missing: A cache layer (Redis/Memcached) is essential for fast redirects.
feedbackTemplates.present: Excellent! Cache will dramatically improve redirect latency.
feedbackTemplates.insufficientReplicas: Consider adding cache replicas for high availability.

feedbackTemplates.missing: You need a database to persist URL mappings.
feedbackTemplates.present: Good choice! Database will reliably store all URL mappings.
feedbackTemplates.insufficientReplicas: For high availability, consider adding read replicas.

feedbackTemplates.missing: Since you selected analytics, add a message queue for async event processing.
feedbackTemplates.present: Smart! Async processing with queue won't slow down redirects.

feedbackTemplates.missing: Analytics requires workers to process click events asynchronously.
feedbackTemplates.present: Good! Workers will process analytics without blocking redirects.

feedbackTemplates.missing: Add a Rate Limiter component to enforce request throttling.
feedbackTemplates.present: Excellent! Rate limiter will protect against abuse.
Great thinking! CDN provides global low-latency access.
Good! Load balancer enables horizontal scaling.

criticalPaths.name: Redirect Path (Read)
criticalPaths.description: The critical path from user request to URL redirect

feedbackTemplates.missing: Your redirect path is missing critical components (Service or Cache).
feedbackTemplates.present: Perfect! Your redirect path flows through cache for fast lookups.
feedbackTemplates.suboptimal: Consider optimizing the redirect path to reduce latency.

criticalPaths.name: Analytics Processing Path
criticalPaths.description: Async path for processing click analytics

feedbackTemplates.missing: Analytics should use async processing: Service → Queue → Workers.
feedbackTemplates.present: Excellent async design! Analytics won't slow redirects.
feedbackTemplates.suboptimal: Analytics path looks good but verify it's fully decoupled from main flow.

architecturePatterns.name: Cache-Aside Pattern
architecturePatterns.description: Service checks cache first, falls back to DB on miss, then updates cache

feedbackTemplates.missing: Implement cache-aside pattern: Service → Cache → DB for fast redirects.
feedbackTemplates.present: Excellent! Cache-aside pattern will deliver sub-50ms redirects.
feedbackTemplates.incorrect: Cache should sit between Service and DB, not as a parallel path.

architecturePatterns.name: Async Event Processing
architecturePatterns.description: Click events sent to queue for async processing by workers

feedbackTemplates.missing: Use async pattern for analytics: Service → Queue → Workers.
feedbackTemplates.present: Perfect async design! Main redirect flow stays fast.
feedbackTemplates.incorrect: Workers should consume from queue, not connect directly to Service.

architecturePatterns.name: Rate Limiting Layer
architecturePatterns.description: Rate limiter sits between API Gateway and Service

feedbackTemplates.missing: Place rate limiter between API Gateway and Service.
feedbackTemplates.present: Good! Rate limiter guards your service from abuse.
feedbackTemplates.incorrect: Rate limiter should be inline, not a side path.

architecturePatterns.name: Horizontal Scaling
architecturePatterns.description: Multiple replicas of critical components with load balancer

feedbackTemplates.missing: With 10K+ RPS, you need horizontal scaling (Load Balancer + replicas).
feedbackTemplates.present: Excellent! Load balancing enables handling high traffic.
feedbackTemplates.incorrect: Verify your Service has multiple replicas to benefit from load balancer.
